concerns.text: Task T017 implements `np.log1p` (log(x+1)) but spec FR-003 explicitly mandates a 'natural log (ln)' transformation. `log1p` is mathematically distinct from `ln` for non-zero values and violates the strict data processing requirement.

Task T038 implements sensitivity analysis on missing value thresholds (0.05, 0.10). Spec FR-003 and Assumptions state datasets with >10% missing values are REJECTED, not imputed at varying thresholds. This task silently relaxes the hard rejection constraint unless the spec is amended to allow robustness checks.

Spec FR-010 and Plan Step 2.4 mandate Leave-One-Dataset-Out (LODO) validation using external datasets. Tasks T009.1/T009.2 only implement ingestion/management stubs. No task exists to actually fetch multiple distinct real-world datasets or execute the LODO loop on them, making the FR-010 deliverable impossible to achieve as specified.

Task T019.1 places KEGG mapping logic in `code/data/preprocess.py`. Spec FR-012 and Plan Step 2.5 designate `code/analysis/pathway.py` for biological validation and KEGG mapping. This structural drift couples data cleaning with domain analysis, violating the architectural constraint defined in the plan.
